titleStmt.title: The Minister in Finland ( (Schoenfeld) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister in Finland ( Schoenfeld ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
267. My telegram No. 266 today. Following is text of my formulation, made at President Ryti’s request, of his remarks to me today:
“In answer to my inquiry as to the policy of the Finnish Government in the present situation of hostilities with the Soviet Union, His Excellency President Ryti stated, and authorized me so to inform the President of the United States, that Finland was a co-belligerent with Germany in those hostilities and was seeking to achieve purely Finnish aims. The strategic position of Finland after the conclusion in 1940 of the previous hostilities with the Soviet Union was dangerously vulnerable and it would be the purpose of Finland to correct that condition for her own protection only. Aside from necessary military understandings, in connection with the parallel operations now proceeding in common with the German forces, no political agreements exist between the Finnish and the German Governments. In view of the restrictions upon imports of food as a result of the action of the British Government, Finland welcomed the expected shipment of 25,000 tons of grain and other material aid including lubricants from Germany. It is the view of President Ryti that the defeat of the Soviet Army and the complete crushing of Bolshevism as a consequence thereof are in the interest of the whole world, and particularly of Finland. But the Finnish people have not departed and have no intention of departing from their established national system of political and social organization.”
